results.rendered_html:
vi-vadha or vī-vadha, m. (prob. fr. √vadh = vah; cf. vadhu) a shoulder-yoke for carrying burdens,  TĀr. ;  ĀśvGṛ.  &c. (cf. vi- and sav˚);
a store of grain or hay, provisions &c.,  Kāv. ;  Kām. ;  Pañcat. ;
a partic. Ekāha,  Vaitān. ;
a road, highway,  L.  (vīv˚,  Pat.  on  Pāṇ. ii, 3, 12 , Vārtt. 1);
a ewer, pitcher,  W. ;
the income which a king obtains from his subjects,  L.
